main.q: Which film won the first Best Picture Oscar (1929)?
main.lesson: Wings, a WWI aviation drama, won the first Best Picture statuette — back when the category was called 'Outstanding Picture'.

main.q: Which Beatles album was the last one the band recorded together?
options: Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band
main.lesson: Abbey Road (1969) was the final album they recorded as a group. Let It Be was released later but most of it was recorded earlier.

main.q: What's the name of the fictional kingdom in Black Panther?
main.lesson: Wakanda first appeared in Fantastic Four #52 (1966), seven years before being introduced in Black Panther's own comic line.

main.q: Which TV show ended with the line "We were on a break!" as a long-running gag?
main.lesson: Ross's 'we were on a break' became one of the most-quoted lines in sitcom history — referenced repeatedly across all 10 seasons of Friends.

main.q: Which musician's album Thriller is the best-selling album of all time?
main.lesson: Thriller (1982) has sold around 70 million copies — partly thanks to the 14-minute music video that effectively invented MTV's golden era.

bonus.q: Which of these video games sold over 300 million copies?
bonus.lesson: All three have crossed 300 million units sold — Tetris through its dozens of versions, Minecraft as the bestselling single game, GTA V across multiple console generations.

bonus.q: What's the name of the dragon in The Hobbit?
bonus.lesson: Smaug, the last great fire-drake of Middle-earth, hoards the dwarves' treasure under the Lonely Mountain.
